stories.headline: Judge spares teenage rapists jail time, sparking outrage from victim and attorney general review.
stories.body:
A girl who was raped by two teenage boys has told the BBC that a judge's decision to spare them jail sentences was like a "rock straight in my face".

The attorney general is to review the sentence given by Judge Nicholas Rowland, who had said on Thursday he wanted to avoid "criminalising" the "very young" boys.

Read more:

stories.headline: Elon Musk highlights Neuralink as major breakthrough for mind-controlled tech and medical miracles.
stories.body:
Elon Musk just said Neuralink is a much bigger breakthrough than most people realize

It’s now enabling people to control computers with their minds, helping paralysis patients regain independence, and potentially allowing blind people to see again

We’re entering an era where technology is starting to achieve things humanity once considered impossible - and what can be described as “Jesus-level miracles”

stories.headline: Scientists use new tracking technology to study monarch butterfly migrations.
stories.body: Monarch butterflies make one of the most extraordinary migrations in the natural world, often traveling thousands of miles across North America. Now, scientists are using new tracking technology to get a greater understanding of those journeys. https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/scientists-use-new-technology-to-track-individual-monarch-butterfly-migrations

stories.headline: French doctors implant lab-grown human trachea made from patient's stem cells.
stories.body:
🚨 SCIENTISTS GREW A HUMAN AIRWAY USING STEM CELLS

A medical breakthrough in France is turning science fiction into reality.
Doctors successfully created a living human trachea using a patient’s own stem cells and implanted it into the body. The lab-grown airway was built on a donor trachea scaffold, then slowly transformed into living tissue the body could recognize as its own.

After surgery, blood vessels grew into the implant and the patient was able to breathe through the regenerated airway.
This technology could one day save people suffering from severe airway damage caused by cancer, trauma, or long-term ventilation.

Source : Institut Pasteur & Hôpital Foch. Medical research on tissue-engineered tracheal reconstruction and regenerative airway transplantation.

stories.headline: Rep. Tim Burchett says Pentagon UFO releases making disclosure mainstream
stories.body:
NEW EXCLUSIVE:
Is Pentagon’s release of UFO files waking Congress up? “Absolutely,” Burchett tells us. "Been great”

Ask a Pol UAP asks:
Since the Pentagon started releasing the ‘UFO files,’ have other members of Congress been talking to you more about it? Like, has it made it a little more mainstream?

Key Burchett:
“Yeah. Oh, absolutely,” Rep. Tim Burchett exclusively tells Ask a Pol UAP.
 ”Thanks to y’all, people like yourself, it’s been great.”

What’s next? Another hearing?
“We’ve had enough hearings. We just need disclosure,” Burchett says.
